question: Will CPI rise more than 0.9% in March 2026?
reasoning: The March 2026 CPI data has already been officially released by the Bureau of Labor Statistics. <cite index="4-1,4-7">The BLS report shows the Consumer Price Index for All Urban Consumers (CPI-U) increased 0.3% in March on a seasonally adjusted basis</cite>. This is well below the 0.9% threshold required for YES resolution. The market should resolve to NO with 100% certainty since the official data shows only 0.3% monthly increase, far short of the 0.9% needed.
key_factors: Official BLS data already released showing 0.3% March CPI increase
Market resolution criteria requires >0.9% monthly increase
Actual result (0.3%) is less than half the required threshold
No possibility of data revision changing outcome this significantly
risks: Potential data revisions (though highly unlikely to change 0.3% to >0.9%)
Market maker error in not updating to reflect released data
